key_insight: A locking-free mixed finite element formulation for elasticity eigenproblems can be built purely from pseudostress, without enforcing symmetry, while still recovering displacement by postprocessing.
engineering_relevance: This enables more robust and reliable eigenanalysis for nearly incompressible or locking-prone solid mechanics problems in 2D and 3D.
method: Formulate the eigenproblem exclusively with the pseudostress tensor in a mixed setting.
Discretize using tensor-valued standard finite element families in H(bdiv).
Prove convergence, a priori error estimates, and a posteriori reliability/efficiency under non-compact operator theory.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical analysis plus numerical tests on multiple geometries and material setups validating convergence and the proposed error estimator.
watch_item: The method relies on postprocessing to recover displacement, so implementation accuracy and solver setup for the mixed pseudostress system are important.
paper_title: A locking free mixed FEM based on a pure pseudostress based formulation for the elasticity eigenproblem
